title: 20 Quantum Cheat Codes That I Wish I Knew In My 20's
description: Join Sandeep as he shares his 20 life-changing cheat codes that took him from being homeless in Mumbai to becoming a monk, an MIT graduate, and a multi-millionaire entrepreneur.
transcript:
I'm going to share with you the 20 cheat
codes that took me from being homeless
in Mumbai to a monk to an MIT grad to a
multi-millionaire entrepreneur I've been
the CEO the Chief Operating Officer and
Senior executive at tech companies that
created over 60 billion dollar of value
nowadays I advise startups billion doll
companies private Equity firms and I've
learned that succeeding in life is
actually quite simple right no it's not
who are we kidding when
is hard that's why we need cheat codes
so let's just get into it my first cheat
code saving is not investing investing
can seem hard and complicated but it
doesn't have to be here is the power of
investing early if you put just $5,000
into an S&P 500 Index every year
starting in your 20s by the time you
retire in your 60s you'll have $1.4
million that is the magic of com
compound growth the trick is you have to
stay in the market don't panic when the
market starts throwing a tantra and I
learned it the hard way in 2008 you
remember during the financial crisis the
world was on the brink of collapse the
S&P 5,000 dropped almost 50% I lost 30%
of my investment and I panicked and I
pulled the rest of it out of the market
but when the market started bouncing
back just after a year 2 I refuse to go
back in if I had done absolutely nothing
and stayed in the market my net worth
today would be 40% higher so two things
don't try to time the market and don't
try to beat the market don't chase
crypto and meme stocks and single stocks
and options and derivatives a lot of
fancy stuff that doesn't work for retail
investors just stick to two rules number
one put it in an index fund and number
two and this is the hard part stay in
the market touch nothing my second cheat
code is the most controversial stop
following your passion passion doesn't
lead to success it works the other way
around a study at a Canadian University
found that only 4% of students could
make money following their passion 96%
of us can't turn our passion into a real
career that raises a powerful question
though right what happened to all that
do what you Love Stuff well they say you
never work a day in your life if you do
what you love when I talk to Young
professionals many of them believe that
if you follow your passion money will
come but here's the truth people are
passionate about creative things like
art music photography painting and those
things don't pay the bills don't focus
on finding your passion focus on
building it by being great at something
passion will follow follow you next up
is three secrets to success practice
practice practice do you know why Taylor
Swift and Kobe Bryant succeeded when
others failed because persistence always
pays I know countless musicians from
Berkeley School of Music and these are
some of the most talented people on the
planet and yet they still put in 8 to 10
hours every single day practicing the
hard stuff stuff that makes their
fingers bleed and their bodies ache
Excellence doesn't come naturally it is
built through practice I'll tell you one
thing that I do well I can sit still for
hours and hours and be focused on deep
work without distraction without boredom
without eating without drinking now
that's my way that's my superpower yours
will be different but the key is to find
it because banking on Talent alone is
like having a Ferrari with no engine
beautiful to look at but it's not going
anywhere so ask yourself what skills can
you practice in hone that are
monetizable now you might be in school
or you might be working at a job without
any unique or marketable skills but
that's okay start today and commit to
becoming amazing at something that
brings us to cheat code number four you
need to treat your career like an
Investment Portfolio a collection of
skills and connections that compound
over time if you're working a corporate
job in your 20s this is why they're
treating you so bad I started at the
bottom of the bottom my first job in
Boston was a help desk guy at a 600
people systems consulting firm and
picture this a small dark room no
windows everything is crammed with
cables and spare parts and servers and
there are three other employees with me
and all four of us we smelled bad and we
got yelled at a lot but you know what
what I was having the time of my life
why because I was getting paid to learn
everything about technology so I would
work for 14 15 hours a day and I'll go
home and I'll still work I'll work
weekends I wanted to know everything
about operating systems about networking
protocols not just hard skills but soft
skills like Communications collaboration
and I wasn't just building skills I was
building connections I would take extra
projects and by the end of those 5 years
I think I put in like 15,000 hours in
building skills and connections and it
paid off I was promoted to be the
program manager and I got my own office
and less than 20 years from my first
promotion I became the CEO of an epic
tech company now if you're starting out
you won't have autonomy you will be
mistreated you will be micromanaged but
treated like a gift be a learning
chameleon soak up everything you can
because if you want to make more money
than others then you have to offer more
value than others my next cheat code
move where the opportunities are if you
want to grow your career then you need
to move where the action is I made that
mistake when I was young I moved to New
York City way too late after being in
Boston for 16 years and of course the
moment I moved to New York that was a
game Cher for me and still today I
question whether I should have moved to
San Francisco when I had the opportunity
in the early 2000s I think that was a
lack of foresight on my part here's the
thing you can't grow your network and
your career by sitting on Zoom calls in
your pajamas of course there are certain
jobs where being remote is okay like
legal or software engineering or field
sales but even in those roles if you are
ambitious and you want to grow you have
to show up you got to be seen because
the exposure you get by being in the
office collaborating with others solving
problems together resolving conflict
face Toof face will teach you skills
that remote work will not next up is
number six understand the difference
between equity and salary the top 1%
don't chase salaries they Chase equity I
always say salary buys you Furniture
Equity buys your future Equity is
exponential you'll own an unlimited
upside and of of course listen N9 out of
10 times you'll swing and you'll miss
happened to me so many times I had stock
options in every company that I joined
some of them failed some of them did
well but that didn't move my needle but
I kept swinging and when I hit it that
was a rain making event it changed my
life and there is another hidden benefit
when you own Equity no matter how small
you start thinking like an owner and
that shift changes the way you approach
your career how you approach your work
real wealth won't come from chasing
paychecks so have an ownership mindset
and try to get some Equity whenever you
can cheat code number seven humble
yourself and get a mentor I waited until
40 to get a mentor don't make the same
mistake yes I waited too long before I
got real career and business mentors
until then it was like trying to
navigate the career mindfield
blindfolded I didn't know what I was
doing once I found the right mentors my
career took off 10 years just 10 years
after I started getting mentored by one
of the most amazing CEOs I became the
CEO and I got to work alongside some of
the most brilliant people on the planet
and do I think that was just a mere
coincidence no not at all having a
mentor is like having the ultimate cheat
code for The Game of Life and by the way
don't fall for those executive coaches
that charge you for advice they're all
fake find real mentors who are invested
in you read a great book it's called
trillion dooll coach it's about Bill
Campbell and is amazing and it's
inspiring cheat code number eight is to
have an emergency fund so you're ready
for whatever life throws at you if you
got laid off this year and didn't have
an emergency fund you probably
struggling to pay your bills and that's
so common a few months ago a friend of
mine was driving home from work and
suddenly he felt like the right side of
his face went numb and he pulled over he
called 911 they rushed him to the
hospital they did all the tests and then
they found it he had a brain tumor and
it was so heartbreaking he's in his
mid-40s and he has a lovely family there
was no warning no rhyme no reason could
happen to any of us right and you have
to expect the unexpected like medical
bills or losing our jobs so try to save
three to 6 months of your expenses in an
emergency fund and please don't touch it
unless and until you have an emergency
cheat code number nine is called forming
your own FBI this framework has saved me
hundreds of thousands of dollars last
year in our case FBI is our financial
Bureau of Investigation first where is
your money going if you don't know where
your money is going how can you control
it second categorize your cash into four
buckets I call them four EAS Emergency
Essentials equity and enjoyment
emergency fund savings for a rainy day
your safety net Essentials the everyday
expense like electricity gas rent Equity
is your Investments how you will build
long-term wealth and enjoyment well
that's the fun stuff vacations Hobbies
entertainment but here's the cat you
only fund the fourth bucket at after the
first three are covered now the easiest
way to build your FBI is to use apps
like mint or good budget but they help
you track and categorize your expenses
next up is cheat code number 10 forget
the American dream unless you want
American debt stop chasing status
symbols like designer clothes it makes
you look broke I was 36 when we bought
our first home my wife and I were dinks
double income no kids and we made sure
that our annual mortgage was about 22%
of our annual expenses and we knew that
we could comfortably manage the mortgage
now home buying is the biggest debt that
you don't need when you in your 20s and
same goes for the credit card debt so
many of us spend so much on designer
Brands using credit cards in the US the
average credit card interest rate is 22%
sometimes it's even 28 8% so say no to
designer sneakers and Status symbols
don't let Brands Define you so the cheat
code is simple wealth isn't about what
you buy it's about what you keep my 11th
cheat code is to get financially
literate Einstein called compound
interest the eighth wonder of the world
there are two books on finance that I
highly recommend one is psychology of
money and the other is algebra of wealth
these books aren't the get-rich quick
nonsense that you get pedal every day
they focus on your mindset on the
discipline and the long-term financial
principles now why does that matter it
matters because financial literacy is
the best investment you can make in
yourself it's the shield that protects
you from bad decisions and financial
traps so start with three things learn
how compounding Works convince yourself
that it is really magic two learn how
credit scores work remember your credit
profile matters more than your dating
profile and number three how taxes work
say out a trouble trust me prison food
doesn't taste great cheat code number 12
automate every financial transaction you
have if you hate saving money this one
thing will make it so much easier when I
was in my 20s I wish I had done things
differently I wish I had let technology
take care of my finance you can learn
from my mistakes and do three things
first automate savings and investment do
that before your paycheck hits your
hands I regret not investing in my 20s I
regret not letting the idea of
compounding work as magic second
automate your bills set up automatic
payments for everything utilities credit
cards subscriptions that way you'll
never miss a payment or get hit by a
late fee third automate your spending
control use technology to put guard
rails around your discretionary spending
that brings us to number 13 go analog
Doom scrolling is killing your critical
thinking skills in the 1970s about 40%
of high school seniors read at least six
books a year and 12% of them didn't read
any today those numbers have flipped no
one wants to read anything we are a
distracted generation but there is hope
a 2017 study found that even a one weak
detox from social media can improve your
attention span and cognitive function
and that's because deep reading and
immersion helps you think critically
engage in complex ideas and even expand
your vocabulary in a world where
everybody is distracted your ability to
be focused will be your superpower cheat
code number 14 is to think of failure as
your friend I know I sound like a
Hallmark card but it is so true the most
success uccessful people we know are the
people that fail the most take me for
example I've been fired not once not
twice but three times in my career and
it wasn't pretty each time it was on the
spot firing no warning I didn't even see
it coming and it was brutal they would
say we're letting you go we'll send your
stuff in boxes later go spend time with
your family and I was like I don't want
to go and my family is the last thing I
want to see when I get fired and being
fired is tough each time I was
unemployed it lasted for more than 6
months and every day it felt like a
struggle reaching into my network or
talking to recruiters even talk to my
friends it was hard I felt embarrassed I
felt like I was a failure but here's
what I've learned failure is not our
Tormentor it's our teacher if you learn
from it it will prepare you for Success
cheat code number 15 is a hard one don't
be afraid way to ask for help there is a
reason they call success a team sport
fear was my biggest Kryptonite for years
it was like this invisible force field
that trapped me into mediocrity and I
was so fearful of rejection I was
paralyzed and now of course I'm old and
I'm Shameless and I embrace the idea
that I will be rejected Ed Chiran was
rejected by every single record label
they thought he was too Ginger or too
acoustic or too nerdy looking he faced
rejections even while performing he'd
get booed and hackled but now he's
selling out stadiums and singing with
Beyonce so someone doesn't like you so
what keep moving until someone does like
you find your tribe surround yourself
with people who inspire you who support
you who challenge you as Dumbledore said
help will always be given to those who
ask for it next up is number 16 don't
play the victim card this is why I'm
friends with the people who fired me I
have stayed in touch with some of the
bosses who let me go a few of them are
still good friends we've even served as
references for each other supported each
other's careers and even had a few
laughs about the day I got fired and
it's the same the other way around in my
career I had to let go a lot of people
and I've tried to stay connected with
them why because business is never
perfect personal when you play the
victim card the only person you hurt is
yourself world keeps moving on and you
stay stuck remember world is not
interested in being fair to us life is
too short to hold grudges my 17th cheat
code is the most important decision you
will make is who you go home to every
day if you end up with the wrong partner
you will be emotionally bankrupt this is
the one thing that I feel I got right in
my early 20s I dated a lot and when I
found the right person then we both took
it slow we spent six to seven years
together living together before deciding
to get married and I was in my 30s then
and by then we understood how we both
thought about life how we would have
tough conversations how would we resolve
conflicts with love and respect and
today she is still my best friend that
one decision has made my life richer in
every way possible now here's something
to remember our brain operates using two
types of thinking systems system one is
a fast emotional gut reaction this is
what takes over when we're infatuated
when we fall for someone attractive it's
immediate it's exciting it's romantic
but it's not deep system two on the
other hand is the slow rational thinker
is the part of your brain that evaluates
the long-term compatibility and weighs
what really matters in your partner and
when it comes to Falling in Love most of
us use system one cheat code 18 is to
forgive yourself for your past
forgiveness is not about them it's about
you I had a troubling childhood
terrifying in fact my father was
physically abusive I don't think our
family has ever talked about it but
growing up I remember being afraid every
evening I would look at the clock I'd be
watching it and it would be 8:00 p.m.
and the doorbell would ring ring and I
would have a panic attack because I knew
my dad was home and over the years he
told me so many things like I was ugly
or I was stupid and I was worthless and
I believed it I didn't have any
confidence and to be fair I don't know
what kind of depression and mental
health issues my dad was suffering
through anyway I left home when I was a
teenager I ran away uh for a while I was
homeless sleeping on train station
benches in Mumbai later I joined an
asham and decided to become a monk and
I'm sharing all of this because I know
I'm not alone when it comes to daddy
issues and you are not alone either we
all share struggles Tony Stark had daddy
issues Luke Skywalker had daddy issues
even Britney Spears has daddy issues All
of Us carry this cross our past is like
a bag of bricks and we can decide to put
it down please forgive yourself for the
way you let your past Define you my 19th
cheat code is true value is about what
you bring to others your success is the
echo of the lives you improve there is a
profound Native American saying that I
love you live only as long as the last
person who remembers you dies think
about it our lives our careers our
Legacy all of of it boils down to just
one thing the impact we make on other
people's lives as I get older I now know
that everything that matters is about
others I didn't know that when I was
young because it's about The Whispers
the stories people will tell about your
kindness your generosity your love your
impact long after you're gone that
brings us to the last cheat code number
20 your happiness is connected to what
you own and what you desire The Secret
Of Happiness is just one simple equation
when I was at the monk Training Center a
senior monk shared with me something
that changed my perspective forever he
was actually a physician by training a
very successful man who became a monk
gave up everything he's probably in his
80s now but still as wise and insightful
as ever he had a simple equation H is
equal to O / D happiness is equal to
what you own o divided by what you
desire D if you own more than you desire
the top number will get larger than the
bottom number and your happiness will
increase but if your desires exceed what
you own your happiness will diminish our
desires can be infinite but the things
we can own are always going to be finite
even for the richest man on this planet
that is a fact and so the key is to
manage your desires if your desires are
exactly equal equ to what you own then
you will find that perfect Unity of joy
and happiness I think it's a brilliant
equation and I will leave you with my
favorite part when your desires approach
zero your happiness goes to Infinity if
you like this video please subscribe to
my channel for more content like this
I'll be posting every week
